Title: Kickoff & Scope Confirmation
Description: Hold remote kickoff meeting with Aranda Tooling point of contact to confirm engagement scope, timeline, deliverables, and communications plan. Walk Client through the preliminary findings context (private keys embedded in historical backups, missing Meraki MS layer in prior export set, management-plane inconsistencies) so expectations for the assessment output are aligned. Capture Client business priorities that should influence severity ranking (e.g., OT/production uptime, guest isolation, printer segmentation, administrative trust boundaries). Document the agreed scope statement, attendees, and action items in kickoff notes.

Title: Meraki Access Provisioning & Validation
Description: Coordinate with a named Aranda administrator to provision read-only Meraki Dashboard access or a time-limited read-only Meraki API key covering the organization and network(s) containing the MX firewall, MS switches, and any MR access points for 13950 Yorba Avenue, Chino, CA. Validate that access reaches the correct organization/network and that the requested read scopes are present. Do not accept shared dashboard passwords transmitted by email. Confirm API key expiry date and log when it must be rotated or revoked at the close of the engagement.

Title: Inventory Validation & Topology Confirmation
Description: Reconcile the Meraki organization device inventory (MX, MS, MR) with the previously supplied non-Meraki switch set (four Aranda-branded Cisco SMB configs — arandaswitch01 through arandaswitch04 — and six Netgear Smart/Plus exports — ArandaNetgear2/3/4/11/14/15). Cross-reference switch management IPs (10.28.1.10, 10.28.1.20, 10.10.0.x range) against the supplied topology diagram to confirm physical placement. Identify the central switch at 10.28.0.2 and confirm whether it corresponds to an MS device in the aggregation/core role. Document any devices present in Dashboard but missing from the config set, or vice versa.

Title: Discovery Memo & Evidence-Gap Log
Description: Produce the Phase 1 discovery memo summarizing confirmed scope, device inventory, access method, and any remaining data-collection items. Create an evidence-gap log identifying any Meraki data items not yet accessible (e.g., switch access policies, DHCP server policy, link aggregations, Layer 3 interfaces if present). The memo and gap log become the baseline for Phase 2 analysis and give Aranda a clear record of what was reviewed versus what remained out of reach.

Title: Meraki MX Firewall Configuration Review
Description: Perform a read-only configuration review of the Meraki MX firewall covering: addressing and VLAN definitions, appliance port assignments, static routes, Layer 3 and Layer 7 firewall rules, NAT translations, DHCP server scopes and reservations, WAN uplink posture (primary/secondary, failover behavior), site-to-site VPN configuration, and any SD-WAN or content-filtering policies that affect segmentation. Validate that inter-VLAN routing is in fact on the MX (as believed from the April 9 clarification) and document any Layer 3 ownership that resides elsewhere. Capture findings with severity, business impact, and recommended action candidates.

Title: Meraki MS Switch Configuration Review
Description: Perform a read-only configuration review of the Meraki MS switch estate covering: global switch settings (management VLAN, STP/RSTP, QoS, multicast, MTU, storm control), per-port configuration (mode, VLAN, native VLAN, allowed VLANs, PoE, schedules, tags), link aggregation groups, access policies, switch ACLs, DHCP server policy / ARP inspection settings, and any Layer 3 interfaces if enabled. Specifically confirm or refute whether the MS layer occupies the aggregation/core role suggested by the topology diagram (central switch 10.28.0.2). Capture findings with severity, business impact, and recommended action candidates.

Title: Non-Meraki Switch Configuration Analysis
Description: Analyze the supplied non-Meraki switch configuration exports: arandaswitch01 (10.28.1.10 — primary office distribution edge, STP priority 4096, widest VLAN/SVI set), arandaswitch02 (10.28.1.20 — downstream office switch), arandaswitch03 (10.28.1.30 — lightly configured IDF), arandaswitch04 (minimal, production status unclear), and the six Netgear Smart/Plus devices in the 10.10.0.0/16 management space. Review VLAN design, trunk/native VLAN consistency, local admin account posture, management IP/default-gateway inconsistency (10.10.0.1 vs 10.28.0.1 split), STP behavior, and configuration hygiene. Flag the embedded private keys and certificates identified in the preliminary review as Critical.

Title: Wireless & SSID Alignment Review
Description: If Meraki MR access points are in use at the site, review SSID-to-VLAN mapping for office, guest, and any purpose-specific SSIDs. Validate local-LAN access policy, per-SSID firewall rules, AP uplink configuration, and guest isolation enforcement. Confirm that wireless segmentation aligns with the intended office and guest design. If no MR is present, document that and note any third-party wireless observed and the implications for segmentation assurance.

Title: End-to-End Segmentation Validation
Description: Validate that the intended segmentation is enforced end-to-end across the Meraki MX/MS layer and the non-Meraki switches. Confirm office (VLAN 20), office wireless (VLAN 30), guest Wi-Fi (VLAN 40), office services (VLAN 50), IoT (VLAN 60), printers (VLAN 70), OT management (VLAN 100), OT computers (VLAN 110), and OT printers (VLAN 120) are carried, trunked, and gatewayed consistently. Identify any gap between defined VLANs and actual end-to-end enforcement. Document any OT-to-office bridging risk, printer/IoT exposure, or guest-to-internal leak vectors.

Title: Security & Hardening Baseline Review
Description: Review security and hardening posture across the assessed devices. Assess local admin account model (named vs shared, consistency across switches), SSH key/certificate posture (including the private keys observed embedded in historical exports), management-plane exposure (where management interfaces are reachable from), logging/SNMP/syslog configuration, NTP/time consistency, firmware version consistency, and backup handling practices. Flag credential rotation, key regeneration, and secure backup handling as Phase 3 remediation inputs. Cyber-risk items receive an elevated severity rating per the preliminary review.

Title: Risk-Ranked Technical Findings Register
Description: Consolidate all Phase 2 observations into a single technical findings register. Each finding includes: finding title, severity (Critical / High / Moderate / Low), affected device(s), business impact, evidence reference, and recommended action. Expected baseline includes: Critical — embedded private keys/certificates in historical backups; High — inconsistent management addressing, privileged-account standardization gap, Meraki MS coverage validation; Moderate — segmentation enforcement ambiguity, non-standard trunk/native VLAN combinations, operational standards drift; Moderate — mixed small-business/Plus-class vendor stack support overhead. Register format: PDF + XLSX.

Title: 30/60/90-Day Remediation Roadmap
Description: Produce a prioritized remediation roadmap covering: Immediate (first 72 hours) — treat historical backups as sensitive, rotate switch admin credentials, regenerate/replace exposed SSH keys and local certificates, restrict access to archives, retire unused devices; 30-day stabilization — normalize management network standard (addressing, VLAN, gateway, NTP, logging, SNMP, naming, backup method), build definitive port map, validate trunk/native VLAN definitions, align on named administrative access, establish secure backup handling and change control; 90-day strategic — long-term switching standard decision (retain or replace Netgear Plus/Smart), consolidate management/monitoring into documented MSP support model, end-to-end segmentation policy review, source-of-truth topology maintenance, post-remediation validation assessment. Remediation labor is explicitly out of scope of this SOW and would be quoted separately.

Title: Device Inventory & Topology Documentation
Description: Produce the normalized device inventory covering Meraki MX (model, serial, firmware, WAN posture), Meraki MS (model, serial, firmware, role, management IP), Meraki MR (if applicable), and non-Meraki switches (model, management IP, firmware, role). Produce the validated topology document that reconciles the supplied diagram with the actual Dashboard-confirmed aggregation/core placement. Note any devices that remain unclassified (e.g., arandaswitch04 production-status unknown, Netgear Plus devices with binary-only exports).

Title: Executive Summary Drafting
Description: Draft the executive summary suitable for non-technical leadership review. Lead with the bottom-line: network is functional and segmented but not yet at a fully standardized MSP or enterprise support standard. Highlight the Critical credential-hygiene finding, the Meraki MS validation outcome, the segmentation posture, and the vendor-consolidation question. Close with the 30/60/90-day roadmap summary and the Technijian-recommended next steps. Keep to one page of narrative plus a one-page risk/findings summary table.

Title: Final Deliverable QA & Packaging
Description: Perform quality review and formatting pass on all Phase 3 outputs: executive summary (PDF), technical findings register (PDF + XLSX), 30/60/90-day remediation roadmap (PDF), device inventory and topology (PDF), and Meraki data-collection checklist / gap log (final). Confirm severity ratings, evidence references, and recommended-action wording are consistent across documents. Package the deliverable set with a cover memo for Client.

Title: Executive Readout Presentation
Description: Deliver the executive readout to Aranda Tooling leadership remotely (up to 90 minutes). Walk through the executive summary, the top findings ranked by risk, the 30/60/90-day roadmap, and the vendor/standardization decision points. Reserve time for Q&A and to capture Client reaction to proposed priorities. Record key decisions, concerns, and any requested follow-up analysis.

Title: Post-Readout Priorities & Next-Step Memo
Description: Produce the post-readout memo summarizing Client-confirmed priorities, any deferred or de-scoped items, and a list of recommended follow-on engagements (remediation labor, credential rotation execution, Meraki MS standardization, vendor consolidation, wireless survey, etc.) that can be scoped into a separate SOW or Change Order. Close out the engagement: revoke the time-limited Meraki API key if issued, confirm deliverable acceptance, and issue the Phase 4 invoice.
